query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "perception or prejudice: can mllms go beyond first impressions of pers" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on gender bias in German LLMs, which is a distinct construct from the user's question regarding "first impressions of personality" (perception vs. prejudice) in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). While both involve bias, the specific mechanism (personality perception vs. gender stereotypes), modality (multimodal vs. text-only), and domain (personality traits vs. gender) do not satisfy the criteria for same-mechanism evidence or independent/dependent variable overlap unde
